item.title: Back defence
item.description: Back defense is fun due to the simplicity of it: Handfighting Prevent opponent from establishing strong grips and more importantly prevent them from isolating an arm.

item.title: Granby roll
item.description: When in Turtle and opponent is on side and not applying constant pressure you can roll over shoulder that is on the opponents side and regain Open guard.

item.title: Guard retention drills
item.description: Rotating with opponent On back with legs on defensive posture. Rotate with elbows. This is particularly easy to practice at home as well.

item.title: Knee on belly defence
item.description: Try Always start with side -rule -idea to see if the knee can be loosened.

item.title: Lapel choke defence -rule
item.description: When opponent has from behind your lapel gripped they are always trying to move their elbow behind your back and you should aim to eliminate that movement, either by handfighting or going to a side.

item.title: Lockdown defence
item.description: Based on using your free leg to push down the opponents locking legs. Not particularly flashy or special.

item.title: Mount Defence
item.description: Always start with getting on side.

item.title: Side control defence
item.description: As the side control is a position there is little use in defending it, as you should aim to perform a sweep or escape.

item.title: Turtle defence
item.description: Granby roll towards the opponent or use unnamed move where you: Take outer leg and raise posture Push your leg through under yourself and opponent to gain Open guard Out of the two Granby is flashier and more reliable When opponent is on your front arc you can also try to: Push upwards with both han...
Turtle & Granby roll theme Ankle not hurt more, but overall low energy training.
